# Higher-Kinded-J

> Unifying Composable Effects and Advanced Optics for Java 25+

Higher-Kinded-J is a comprehensive functional programming library for Java providing composable error handling through the Effect Path API, type-safe immutable data navigation through the Focus DSL, for-comprehensions for monadic sequencing, MTL-style capabilities, virtual-thread concurrency, resilience patterns, and compile-time safety tooling.

## Effect Path API

A railway model for computation where success travels one track and failure travels another. Operations like `map`, `via`, `recover`, and `focus` work identically across all effect types.

**Path Types:**
- `MaybePath<A>` - Optional values where absence is normal
- `EitherPath<E, A>` - Typed errors with structured information
- `TryPath<A>` - Wrapping code that throws exceptions
- `ValidationPath<E, A>` - Accumulating ALL errors, not just the first
- `IOPath<A>` - Deferred side effects
- `VTaskPath<A>` - Virtual-thread concurrency (Project Loom)
- `VStreamPath<A>` - Reactive-style virtual-thread streams
- `NonDetPath<A>` - Non-deterministic computation (list monad)
- `IdPath<A>` - Identity wrapper for generic programming
- `OptionalPath<A>` - Java Optional integration
- `LazyPath<A>` - Lazy evaluation
- `ListPath<A>` - List monad
- `StreamPath<A>` - Java Stream integration
- `ReaderPath<R, A>` - Dependency injection
- `WriterPath<W, A>` - Accumulated output
- `WithStatePath<S, A>` - Stateful computation
- `TrampolinePath<A>` - Stack-safe recursion
- `CompletableFuturePath<A>` - Async operations
- `FreePath<F, A>` / `FreeApPath<F, A>` - DSL building
- `GenericPath<M, A>` - Wraps any Kind type as a Path

## For-Comprehensions

Monadic sequencing without deeply nested flatMap/via chains. Three styles:

- **`For`** - Works with any `Monad<M>` and `Kind<M, A>` values. Uses `Tuple2`-based accumulation with up to 12 chained bindings.
- **`ForPath`** - Path-native comprehensions returning Path types directly. Spread-parameter `yield` for readable results. Supports all Path types.
- **`ForState`** - Named-field state comprehensions via `For.toState()` bridge. Eliminates positional tuple access in favour of record field names.

```java
// ForPath example: flat sequential steps instead of nested via() chains
ForPath.from(validateOrder(request))
    .from(order -> reserveInventory(order))
    .from(t -> processPayment(t._1()))
    .yield((order, reservation, payment) -> buildResult(order, payment));

// For -> toState -> ForState: named access for multi-step workflows
For.from(eitherMonad, lookupAddress(request))
    .from(addr -> lookupCustomer(addr))
    .from(t -> buildOrder(t._1(), t._2()))
    .toState(ProcessingState::from)
    .fromThen(s -> enrichOrder(s.order()), ProcessingState::withEnrichedOrder)
    .yield(ProcessingState::toResult);
```

## Advanced Optics

Comprehensive optics implementation for type-safe immutable data access and update:
- **Lens** - Focus on a field in a product type
- **Prism** - Focus on a case in a sum type (sealed interfaces)
- **Iso** - Bidirectional transformation
- **Affine** - Optional focus (zero or one)
- **Traversal** - Multiple focuses in a collection
- **Fold** - Read-only traversal
- **Getter** / **Setter** - Read-only or write-only access
- **At** / **Ixed** - Key-based and index-based access
- **Each** - Access each element in a container
- **IndexedLens** / **IndexedTraversal** / **IndexedFold** - Position-aware optics

## Focus DSL

Type-safe structural navigation integrated with Effect Paths:
- `FocusPath<S, A>` - Exactly one focus
- `AffinePath<S, A>` - Zero or one focus
- `TraversalPath<S, A>` - Zero or more focuses

```java
userService.findById(userId)           // EitherPath<Error, User>
    .focus(UserFocus.address())        // EitherPath<Error, Address>
    .focus(AddressFocus.postcode())    // EitherPath<Error, String>
    .via(code -> validatePostcode(code));
```

## Type Classes

Core abstractions in `hkj-api`:
- **Functor** / **Bifunctor** / **Profunctor** - Mapping
- **Applicative** / **Alternative** / **Selective** - Combining
- **Monad** / **MonadError** / **MonadZero** - Sequencing and error handling
- **Foldable** / **Traverse** - Aggregation and traversal
- **Semigroup** / **Monoid** - Combining values
- **Natural** - Natural transformations between functors
- **Choice** - Choice profunctor for optics

## MTL (Monad Transformer Library)

Capability-based interfaces decoupling effects from concrete transformer stacks:
- **MonadReader<M, R>** - Read-only environment access (`ask`, `local`)
- **MonadState<M, S>** - Stateful computation (`get`, `put`, `modify`)
- **MonadWriter<M, W>** - Accumulated log output (`tell`, `listen`, `pass`)

## Monad Transformers

Stack multiple effects:
- **EitherT<M, E, A>** - Errors over any monad
- **MaybeT<M, A>** - Optionality over any monad
- **OptionalT<M, A>** - Java Optional over any monad
- **ReaderT<M, R, A>** - Environment over any monad (implements MonadReader)
- **StateT<M, S, A>** - State over any monad (implements MonadState)
- **WriterT<M, W, A>** - Logging over any monad (implements MonadWriter)

## Concurrency (Virtual Threads)

Built on Project Loom:
- **VTask** - Lightweight virtual-thread tasks with structured concurrency
- **VStream** - Reactive-style streams on virtual threads with backpressure, throttling, parallel fan-out, and reactive interop
- **Scope** - Structured concurrency scopes with resource management
- **Par** - Parallel combinators for VTask
- **Resource** - Bracket-style resource management

## Resilience Patterns

Production-grade fault tolerance:
- **CircuitBreaker** - Fail-fast with configurable thresholds, metrics, and state transitions
- **Saga** - Distributed transaction compensation with automatic rollback
- **Retry** - Configurable retry with exponential backoff
- **Bulkhead** - Concurrency limiting

## Code Generation

Annotation-driven boilerplate elimination:
- `@GenerateLenses` - Generates Lens optics for record fields
- `@GeneratePrisms` - Generates Prism optics for sealed interface variants
- `@GenerateFocus` - Generates FocusPath accessors
- `@GenerateTraversals` - Generates TraversalPath for collections
- **ForPath step classes** - Steps 2-12 auto-generated for all 10 Path types
- **Processor plugins** for Array, Either, List, Map, Maybe, Optional, Set, Try, Validated

## Compile-Time Safety

- **hkj-checker** - Compiler plugin that detects Path type mismatches at compile time (e.g. using `EitherPath` where `MaybePath` expected)
- **hkj-openrewrite** - Automated code migration recipes

## Installation

```gradle
dependencies {
    implementation("io.github.higher-kinded-j:hkj-core:LATEST_VERSION")
    annotationProcessor("io.github.higher-kinded-j:hkj-processor-plugins:LATEST_VERSION")
}
```

**Modules:**
- `hkj-api` - Type class interfaces (Functor, Monad, etc.)
- `hkj-core` - All implementations (324+ source files)
- `hkj-annotations` - Annotation definitions
- `hkj-processor` - Annotation processor
- `hkj-processor-plugins` - Optics generator plugins
- `hkj-checker` - Compile-time checker plugin
- `hkj-openrewrite` - Code migration recipes
- `hkj-spring:starter` - Spring Boot integration
- `hkj-gradle-plugin` / `hkj-maven-plugin` - Build plugins
- `hkj-bom` - Bill of Materials for version alignment

## Spring Boot Integration

`hkj-spring-boot-starter` provides:
- Return Path types directly from `@RestController` methods
- Jackson serialization for all Path types
- Security context integration
- Actuator health and metrics endpoints

## Documentation

- Main: https://higher-kinded-j.github.io/latest/home.html
- Effect Path API: https://higher-kinded-j.github.io/latest/effect/ch_intro.html
- Optics Guide: https://higher-kinded-j.github.io/latest/optics/optics_intro.html
- Focus DSL: https://higher-kinded-j.github.io/latest/optics/focus_dsl.html
- Resilience: https://higher-kinded-j.github.io/latest/resilience/resilience_intro.html
- Tutorials: https://higher-kinded-j.github.io/latest/tutorials/tutorials_intro.html
- Spring Boot: https://higher-kinded-j.github.io/latest/spring/spring_boot_integration.html
- Examples: https://higher-kinded-j.github.io/latest/examples/ch_intro.html

## Source Code

- Repository: https://github.com/higher-kinded-j/higher-kinded-j
- License: MIT

## When to Use Higher-Kinded-J

Use this library when you need:
- Composable error handling without exception pyramids
- For-comprehensions to flatten nested monadic chains
- Type-safe immutable data updates with optics
- Integration between effect handling and data navigation
- Virtual-thread concurrency with structured scopes
- Resilience patterns (circuit breakers, sagas, retry)
- MTL-style capability abstraction over transformer stacks
- Code generation for lenses/prisms on Java records and sealed interfaces
- Compile-time checking for Path type correctness
- Functional patterns in Spring Boot applications
